rulings.Case Title: JOVICA MAKSIMOVIC et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A. RECONTRUST et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA N.A.,and RECONTRUST COMPANY, N.A.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The Defendant's demurrer is SUSTAINED. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND as to the first, second, third, fifth, and sixth causes of action. As to the first and fifth causes of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts showing Plaintiffs' injury-in-fact and how and why Defendants' actions were unlawful, unfair, or fraudulent within the meaning of section 17200. As to the second and third causes of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts supporting standing to enforce the contract and what specific provisions of the contract were breached by the facts alleged. As to the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege facts supporting cancellation. The demurrer is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEDND as to the fourth cause of action because Civil Code section 2923.55 was not effective when the Notice of Default was filed against Plaintiff. =(501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: SOUTH COUNTY BANK, N.A. VS. FIRST-CITIZENS BANK & TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 7 - PLAINTIFF SOUTH COUNTY BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's motion for preliminary injunction is DENIED. Even assuming the Court could enjoin the alleged breach of contract, Plaintiff has not shown it is likely to prevail on the merits given a reasonable interpretation of its rights under the participation agreement, and Plaintiff has not shown irreparable injury will occur given that any injury would be compensable in damages. =(501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. SHARMAYNE JACKSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintif John Stewart Company'S Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 1- Plaintif John Stewart Company'S Motion For Judgment. Plaintiff's motion for judgment pursuant to stipulation is GRANTED. Defendant breached the stipulation, and Defendant did not file any opposition by the deadline of May 17, 2013. =(501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: AGP 2619 MISSION, LLC VS. LIDIA ZULETA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 6 - DEFENDANT LIDIA ZULETA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is DENIED. There is at least a triable issue of material fact whether a new tenancy was created when Defendant was terminated as resident manager. =(501PJB)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. EDWIN KEITH STEPHEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For An Order To Show Cause Re: Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 4 - DEFENDANT EDWIN STEPHEN's Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re: Sanctions. Off calendar for insufficient notice. The motion was noticed for May 16, 2013 and was served by mail April 22, 2013, so the earliest hearing date would have been May 20, 2013. The failure of notice is not cured by the continuance. =(501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: RINA BELLO VS. JAVIER TENORIO et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 2 - DEFENDANT JAVIER TENORIO and MARIETTA TENORIO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The matter is off calendar per filing of the second amended complaint on May 14, 2013. =(501/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ARTHUR R CAYABYAB et al VS. SANDRA KISSIG et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 22, 2013, line 3 - DEFENDANT SANDRA KISSIG's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion to set aside/vacate judgment is DENIED. Defendant's motion is based exclusively on Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d), arguing that Court lacks jurisdiction. Defendant has not shown Plaintiffs lacked standing, let alone that the Court lacks jurisdiction. On reply, Defendant seeks relief under section 473(b). Even assuming it were proper to consider the request for relief first sought on reply, Defendant has failed to attach a proposed responsive pleading, without which the motion cannot be granted. =(501/PJB)
